desc:
Gently rolling plains stretch out to the east and south for uncounted miles.
Green stalks of grass grow as high as a man's legs, and move in the gentle
breeze. In the distant west, the swiftly moving waters of the Isen sing a
babbling melody day and night. Framing the plains to the north and south, two
majestic mountain ranges rise above the horizon, dark in the north, and in the
south, white as the driven snow.
name: Rolling Meads

desc:
Upon the horizon, far to the north, the Misty Mountains climb into sight. The
land stretches in plains in several directions, except for immediately to the
north, where a ridge rises above the flat lands. Golden wheat seems to grow in
small patches here, being a sure sign that a traveller is entering further into
the plains of Rohan.
name: Plains of Rohan

desc:
Wild wheat grass grows atop the rich Rohan soil. To the west lie additional
clusters of yellow wheat while to the north the grass becomes shorter and
thinner. Mixed among the short grass to the north live several small bundles of
sagebrush. A ramshackle collection of wooden structures can barely be seen on
the horizon to the east.
name: Amidst the Wild Wheat

desc:
Old wooden fences, in urgent need of mending, box in a small piece of land that
connects to a ramshackle village in the north. Inside the fences, the earth has
been worn barren by over-grazing of herd animals. Occasionally a small horse or
cow will wander near the fence, hoping to snag a mouthful of green grass just
beyond its reach. Beyond the fence, a mix of golden grains fights with the lush
grey-green grasses for dominion of this area. The wheat stalks hold their ground
for less than a mile, before surrendering to the sea of green.
name: Fields Of Haron Dur

desc:
In the east stands a small farming village, barely more than a few houses
surrounding a young tree and a well. Flanking the village to the north and south
are small, cultivated fields of wheat, which are adjacent to livestock corrals
for horses and smaller pens for farm animals. A few miles west of the village
entrance, the Great South Road bends and winds its way to the White Mountains in
the south.
name: Entrance to Haron Dur

desc:
Grey-green plains give way to cultivated fields of amber grains and small horse
corrals, as the outskirts of a small village encroach upon the open land. Less
than a mile to the south, the heart of that small village can be seen through
the small wisps of smoke from chimneys. In the northeast, the verdant grasses of
Rohan lay claim to the land for miles, until they reach the forested base of the
Mountains of Mist.
name: Endless Plains

desc:
Vast green plains stretch out in every direction. To the east, the gently
undulating grasslands seem to flow into eternity while in the west a wide, stone
roadway abruptly halts them. Though several miles away, the condition and make
of the road are easily determined. It is an ancient piece of work, as evidenced
by its uneven and weathered edges. Also, in stark contrast to the green
surrounding it, the road is a dingy white, perhaps the effect of years of
exposure to the sun.
name: Endless Plains

desc:
Muted green plains flow for miles in every direction. In the southwest, a large
stone road cuts a wide scar through the gently rolling plains. From this distant
vantage, the road appears to be worn by both weather and time, but its
appearance could be an illusion - a trick played by the light.
name: Endless Plains

desc:
The grass is slightly shorter here than in the surrounding plains. It still
grows just as thick, but appears to have been cut down by repeated grazing.
Small purple flowers grow here in abundance, particularly in areas where the
grass has been eaten down to the bare soil.
name: Rohan Plains

desc:
As the plains of Rohan gently roll in every direction, light and darkness frame
the aptly named gap. Two mountain ranges, opposite in every way, block in the
plains to the north and south. The dark peaks of the Misty Mountains in the
north are shrouded and hidden in permanent clouds and mist. In the south the
white, jagged peaks of Erid Nimrais stand stark and open against the sky.
name: High Plains of Rohan

desc:
This well-worn footpath is expertly hidden by the thick, green grass. Though
heavily travelled, this path is extremely narrow and winding, making it very
difficult to navigate in these massive and seemingly unending plains. The
repeated footfalls of both man and beast keep the path clear and easy to follow
as long as one stays on the barren earth.
name: Eastbound Trail

desc:
The grey-green plains extend past the distant eastern horizon. A dense carpet of
grass covers the land so completely, that no tree or bush obstructs the view for
miles. Occasionally, a small herd of wild horses can be spotted grazing upon the
fertile grasses, but little else breaks up the monotony of the sea of green.
name: Green Meads

desc:
The ridge here is at its lowest point and steadily rises further as its run in a
south-west direction. It is not as rocky or uneven, as the lands just below, or
the terrain further up the ridge to the south. The waters of the Isen flow by to
the west, issuing from the mouth of a mountain valley a few miles to the north.
name: Beginnings of a Ridge through the Plains

desc:
Rocks, boulders and holes in the earth scar the plains. Travel by mount would be
impossible due to the inconsistent terrain. The land appears to continue roughly
to the north, where it slopes away down to the river. South of here the plains
continue, seemingly less marred with rough terrain. Far to the north, the caps
of the Misty Mountains are barely visible in the clouds.
name: Uneven Plains

desc:
The landscape here is rough and marred by rocks and holes. Some lush, green
grass spreads in patches, interspersed between the stony outcrops. To the west,
the land can be seen falling away into a valley, while away to the far north,
the peaks of the Misty Mountains can be discerned. To the south, the ground
slopes downwards into a depression.
name: Stony Flats

desc:
The land dips into a large and shallow bowl-shaped pit. Small fissures are
leading away from the steep eastern side, too narrow to enter, while on the
other sides the slopes are more gradual. The base of the depression is covered
in tough, grey-green grass, occasionally dotted with rocks and pebbles.
name: Depression in the Plains

desc:
A large, rough ridge climbs slowly above the plains here, growing steadily as it
goes. The ground is hard and lined with stones and sudden pits. It sports little
vegetation except for a short covering of hardy grass. To the north, the land
dips again, towards a depression in the plains. Below to the west, bright green
plains roll southward.
name: Ridge through the Plains of Rohan

desc:
Rough grass coats the floor of this minor dale formed by a rugged road to the
north and east as well as the beginning of a lengthy ridge to the west. A few
blackberry bushes grow interspersed among the coarse grass. The harsh grassland
continues to the south.
name: Secluded Glen beside the Trail

desc:
Thriving grass coats the floor of the valley. Mixed in the midst of the grass
are tiny plants with round, smooth stems and a woody root. To the west lie weedy
grasslands with thinning grass littered by rocks. In contrast, lush grass with
abundant shrubbery sits atop the prairie to the north. The pleasant grassland
ends abruptly to the south, becoming stony terrain.
name: Flourishing Grassland near the Outcroppings

desc:
Stiff and rugged bluebunch wheatgrass underfoot slows any traveler throughout
this area. However, the bristly grass and other tough weeds present easier
terrain than the harsh, craggy rocks to the west. Pleasant grassland to the east
provides a stark contrast to the rough terrain in the north and west.
name: Rocky Mead

desc:
Short, rough crabgrass coats the floor of the vale here. In numerous places,
bedrock pushes its way through the grass. The ground slopes sharply to the west
down to a rocky dip on the plains. In the south, several bunches of three-foot
high bushes stand atop a luscious green grass while in the east the green grass
dominates the landscape as far as the eye can see.
name: Lush Fields

desc:
The various thick leaves of several wild plants give this area a sweet smell.
Small insects buzz quietly, lured by sugary nectar secreted by the vegetation.
Beneath the variety of plants, rough grass lines the slope descending from a
ridge to the west. In the south, small clumps of wheat grass lie huddled
together while to the east sit more gently rolling, flat grasslands.
name: Grassy Mead beside the Ridge

desc:
The fertile soil allows the growth of dense meadows. The thick, green grass
provides a cushion for one's feet and excellent nourishment for herbivores that
inhabit the surrounding grasslands. To the south, the grassland ends suddenly in
a wall of impassable thickets. A small village, seemingly abandoned, is scarcely
visible to the east.
name: Grey-green Mead

desc:
Beautiful grass coupled with a large quantity of wild flowers smother the floor
of the prairie. A small incline to the west leads up to more grassland while the
prairie leads gently down a slope to the north to more lush grass and flora.
Impenetrable brush blocks the way southward. Similarly, the prairie ends
abruptly to the east as a small village, seemingly deserted, begins.
name: Entrance to Derelict Village

desc:
The remains of a once-flourishing village lie apparently abandoned here. A stone
well, its sides worn smooth over time, stands in the centre of the village, the
only functioning component of the forsaken village. The buildings provide a
clear sign of the vacant nature of the settlement as entire boards are missing
from the walls of the most structures and only a handful of glass panes remain
in windows. In some places, plants grow on the wooden walls while in other
places holes puncture the buildings.
name: Inside a Forgotten Hamlet

desc:
Ubiquitous weeds sprout from the compacted earth here. At one time, this
courtyard served as the hub of activity for the farmstead. Beyond the doorway to
the north, stalls remain visible while to the south a small entrance leads to a
utility building. The courtyard extends westward where a small well sits in the
centre of the square. Finally, the land to the east served as grazing lands for
the animals housed in the small barn to the north.
name: Weed-filled Courtyard

desc:
Thick shrubs of all shapes and sizes dominate the countryside. So dense is the
vegetation that it is quite possible not a single inch of the ground beneath
ever feels the touch of sunlight. To the south the coverage is so complete that
it would make it impossible for any new seedlings to sprout through. Northeast
of here a a brown trail provides a sharp contrast to this sea of green and a
very faint footpath seems to in one direction lead north from here and then turn
east towards that trail, and in the other head towards a village seen to the
west.
name: Profuse Underbrush

desc:
Countless shrubs lie amalgamated among the rolling meads of grass. The
branching, oval stems of one shrub eventually give way to notched, heart-shaped
leaves covered with grey hair. Another bush with large elliptical leaves on
branched stems possesses yellow, succulent berries. To the south the underbrush
thickens to a point making passage in that direction impossible.
name: Shrub-covered Grasses

desc:
Thriving grasses dominate the landscape. In some places, the grass hinders
passage due to its height and density. A minor bridge crossing a gully dominates
the northern horizon. The land slopes down gently to a shrub-filled glen in the
west and leads upwards to a trail passing in the east.
name: Lush Grasslands

desc:
A large number of stones and clumps of weeds litter the remnants of what was
once the surface of a road. A steep, rocky cliff forces the abrupt end of the
trail to the south. The remains of a small camp show the possible destination of
the trail. A collection of rocks covered by moss and other weeds form a small
circle at the end of the trail, suggesting the location of a campfire in the
past.
name: End of the Decrepit Trail

desc:
The grasses of the rolling meadow stand several feet tall in most places. An
occasional part in the high grass reveals a location where some creature passed.
Similarly flourishing grasslands surround the area, with the exception of a
large yellow patch of grass in the north. A small trail to the west ends when it
meets a steep precipice in the south.
name: Arid Meadows

desc:
Tall shoots of grass surrounded by thick patches of weed cover the flat ground.
The only barren areas of the grass-covered land are some ancient flats of rock
pushing from the ground. Remains of a long since abandoned campfire can be seen
nearby on one of the rocky flats. Far in the east the level grasslands turn into
rows of low hills.
name: Standing Meadows

desc:
The rolling golden-green lands grow verdant with tall and coarse grass. Some
slender trees grow nearby swaying back and forth as if they were unsure of their
place in the grassy landscape. Small bushes and shrubs dot the hilly
grass-covered land. The grasslands become more level to the west and continue in
steady waves in other directions.
name: Rolling Grasslands

desc:
Tall and coarse grass covers the rolling lands. Far to the northeast the lands
turn into bleak marshy plains. A serpentine stream can be seen cutting across
the plains in the east. A particularly lush tract of land, overgrown with
grasses and shrubs, lies before the stream. The rolling grasslands span far to
the west and north.
name: Rolling Grasslands

desc:
Tall and coarse grass, sometimes as high as three feet, covers the moist ground
of this tract of land. Some flowers, mostly blue and violet, grow amidst the
grasses. A stream slowly makes its way across the lands in the east. The part of
the stream in the east looks shallow and fordable. To the north the lands become
marshy and covered with mist while to the west the grassy rolling plains
continue far to the horizon.
name: Lush Vegetation

desc:
Here the stream running from the north becomes shallow and weak as if it was
tired from its passage through the marsh. To the west, the land is overgrown
with grasses and low-lying bushes while to the east level, grassy plains stretch
themselves as far as one can see. To the south the stream quickly becomes deeper
and swifter again as it continues its way into the plains of Rohan.
name: Shallow Stream

desc:
In the west, the waters of the stream running from the north have become shallow
and the stream can be forded. To the south, the stream quickly becomes deeper
and swifter again as it runs towards the grassy plains of Rohan. A mist-covered
marshland lies to the north while in the east the ground gently slopes into
verdant, grassy lowlands.
name: Plains by the Stream

desc:
The verdant lowlands, covered with a carpet of tall grass, expand to the north,
where they gradually rise to the plains above. A long and gentle treeless slope,
dotted with red flowers, rises in the east. The air down in the vale is moist
and rich with the fragrance of the grasslands. Glimmering spots of blue shine
through the grasses, where puddles of water have formed.
name: Grassy Lowlands

desc:
From the verdant lowlands in the west the land climbs up to the wind-beaten
plains in the east, a gentle treeless slope, furrowed with folds and gullies.
Some white and purple flowers dot the grassy slope. A gust of wind sweeping
across the plains carries the faint scent of the grassy meadows. The grassy
plains of the Rohirrim stretch away to the horizon.
name: A Long Treeless Slope

desc:
The empty wind-beaten lands grow scarce with grass, the short turf breaking away
in some places, exposing the barren, heavily textured soil. The wide grey-green
plains stretch far to the southerly lands of the realm of Rohan. To the north
and west the ground slopes down to the plains below, gradually turning into
verdant grasslands.
name: Windy Plains

desc:
The plains are covered with a carpet of short needle-like grass. Some herbs with
broad green leaves grow amidst the grass, adding a sweet fragrance to the air.
In the west the plains rise noticeably towards a line of low downs that sit far
to the southwest. The plains stretch away far to the east towards the distant
river lands.
name: Sparse Grassland

desc:
The grassy lands rise considerably towards a line of low downs visible in the
west. The level grasslands spread out to the edge of sight, white poppies
dotting the green background. Some low bushes grow scattered around the plains.
Towards the south the ground becomes harder and the grass shorter.
name: Plains by the Downs

desc:
Short needle-like grass covers the empty level plains in a green carpet, with
patches of grey in some places where the grass has withdrawn to expose the
under-lying bedrock. The grass-covered plains expand far to the horizon. A
cluster of white and red poppies grows amidst the grasses.
name: Sparse Grasses

desc:
The ground here becomes harder and the grass shorter, in spots the grass has
retreated to reveal the bare soil underneath. Some tufts of taller grass and
patches of white poppies lie scattered around the flatlands. To the south the
land gradually starts to rise towards a wind-beaten stretch of plains, while
elsewhere the level grass-covered plains spread out.
name: Bare Flatlands

desc:
Short grass covers the sloping plains, tufts of taller grass thrusting out from
the hard ground in places. Some white and red poppies dot the green slope,
growing in folds and narrows of the furrowed land. In the south the empty
wind-beaten lands stretch far away to the southerly lands.
name: Rising Plains

desc:
The plains of grass stretch far to the edge of sight. Tall grasses, spotted with
white flowers, cover the lands. To the east the plains gradually rise higher
while in the southwest the plains slope down to verdant lowlands filled with
flowers. Some droppings on the ground hints of the animals that graze the
grasslands.
name: Wide Empty Plains

desc:
The bare flatlands spread out all around. The treeless plains are covered with
tall yellow-green grasses. Far to the north a low humpbacked ridge rises from
the barren grasslands. The white head of Methedras, the last peak of the Misty
Mountains, glimmering in a grey cloud can be seen in the northwest. The wind
carries the faint scent of the grassy plains. Clusters of white flowers grow
amidst the grasses in water-filled dips.
name: Wide Empty Plains

desc:
The grasslands swell up to a line of downs running from north to west. The green
lands are covered with grasses and flowers. The flat plains stretch away from
the downs. The air is soft and has a faint scent of the verdant grasslands. A
clump of bushes grows nearby, its berries already eaten by some animals.
name: Plains by the Downs

desc:
The downs offer a view of the surrounding flatlands stretching away from the
foot of the ridge. The land slopes steeply down to the barren, level plains. The
wind-beaten ground of the mound is hard and covered with short turf. To the
south the lands seem to be more alive and verdant, covered in tall green grass
and dotted with flowers.
name: A Line of Downs

desc:
The plains swell up to a grassy ridge where a line of humpbacked downs stands in
the south. Tufts of grass protrude from the sloping land. The flatlands spread
out far to the north towards the mountains and the great forest. The ground
underfoot is hard and dry, and covered with short turf. Some small stones lies
scattered around the slope.
name: Rising Plains

desc:
In the north a strip of sunken land can be seen, the land sloping down to a deep
and rocky cleft in the plains. The long gentle slopes of grass stretch far to
the east while in the west a lush uncultivated field lies. To the south the
plains gradually rise to meet a line of low downs.
name: Rising Plains

desc:
A barren grass-covered ridge runs from east to south. From the crest of the
ridge a snaking stream can be seen in the west, its waters shimmering against
the green canvas of grass. The land slopes steeply down to the surrounding
plains that expand far to the edge of sight. Beyond the stream in the west a
marshy mist-covered region lies.
name: A Line of Downs

desc:
An impressive view of the surrounding plains opens from the crest of the grassy
ridge that runs here from the north. To the west a stream can be seen cutting
through the marshy mist-covered lands. The land descends steeply in every
direction, the southern slope being the gentlest, furrowed with gullies and
ravines. Far to the north, ten leagues away, looms the shadow of the Fangorn
forest.
name: A Line of Downs

desc:
The grass-covered land swells up to a line of low downs that lie ahead in the
north. To the west a serpentine stream snakes its way to the marshes that lie
southwest from here. Long gentle slopes lead to the grassy lowlands in the
south. Nearby stands a thicket of slender trees and shrubs.
name: Plains by the Downs

desc:
The grassy low-lying lands grow lush with tall grass, a carpet of greens dotted
with red and orange flowers. The lowlands expand all around in a sea of flowing
green grass. To the west lies the marshlands, covered in a veil of silky white
mist. The air here is moist and fragrant. To the north, the land gradually
climbs to the plains and the downs that lie ahead.
name: Grassy Lowlands

desc:
The soft and wet ground yields underfoot, making the progress over the miry land
precarious and tiresome. Sedges and cattails dominate the marshy land, standing
tall and erect with their solid stems and brown furry heads. In the west a
stream slowly snakes through the marshland, glistening through a thin veil of
mist that covers the land.
name: Deceitful Quagmire

desc:
The waters become murky as the stream snakes through the marshland. Mist flows
in from the marsh covering the surface in a thin white blanket. Some cattails
grow on the southern bank of the stream, swaying silently in the breeze. To the
south the deceitful quagmire lies, while in the east the lands gradually turn
into verdant plains again.
name: Stream through the Marsh

desc:
The stream slows down considerably before entering the marshland in the south,
hesitant to leave the pastoral grasslands behind. To the east the lands slowly
rise towards a line of downs while in the west the plains gradually turn into
sodden fields. Few wisps of mist flow in from the marsh, swirling above the
water like snakes.
name: Serpentine Stream

desc:
The narrow stream takes a turn southwards, snaking towards the mist-covered
marshlands ahead. In the east the land rises towards a line of low downs, while
to the north the plains stretch away to the edge of sight, all the way to the
eaves of the great forest. The banks of the stream grow thick with grasses and
shrubs.
name: Serpentine Stream

desc:
The yellow-green land rises slightly to the southeast towards a line of low
downs. The grasses grow lush and tall in the uncultivated lands all around. A
stream cuts across the plains to the west, shimmering in a sea of grass. It
bends slightly eastwards before continuing its snaking way towards the marshes
in the southerly lands.
name: Uncultivated Fields

desc:
The narrow stream gradually slows down as it starts meandering through the
marshland that lies ahead. Some cattails with long flat leaves growing on the
banks bend over the stream. Thin wisps of mist flow in from the sodden lands in
the west, floating above the slowly moving water. The water is muddy, making it
impossible to see the bottom of the stream. Grassy plains expand far to the east
beyond the current.
name: Serpentine Stream

desc:
Thin wisps of mist flow in from the marsh softly covering the stream with a
milky white veil. The stream makes a turn eastwards where it continues its
snaking way through the marsh. Some tufted marsh plants with solid stems grow on
the muddy banks of the current. The water here is muddy, making it hard to see
the bottom of the stream.
name: Serpentine Stream

desc:
The soft miry ground is covered with sedges and grasses that make the passage
through the marsh tedious. A patch of cattails pokes through a thin veil of mist
that covers the marsh, swaying silently in the breeze, as if watching over the
land. In the east a narrow stream snakes its way through the marshland, bending
slightly this way and continuing then on its southward course.
name: Tenacious Marshland

desc:
Thin wisps of mist flow in from the marsh covering the stream in a soft
milky-white blanket. The narrow stream slowly snakes through the miry,
sedge-filled lands. The waters of the stream is muddy, hiding the bottom and
whatever secrets they may hold. A row of cattails gently bends over the stream
on one of the banks.
name: Stream through the Marsh

desc:
The mist-covered waters of the stream run slowly, almost unnoticeably, through
the miry lands southwards. To the south where the stream finally breaks away
from the marsh the waters seem to become more shallow and fordable. The banks of
the stream grow thick with grasses and marsh plants that peek through the mist,
swaying silently as if bidding you farewell.
name: Stream through the Marsh

desc:
The ground underfoot is soft and sodden and makes travelling precarious. Grasses
and cattails cover the land. A thin veil of mist floats above the wet turf of
the marshland. In the east a narrow stream makes its way through the miry lands.
To the west the ground gradually becomes firm and the lands turn into rolling
plains.
name: Sodden Fields

desc:
The rolling hills and meadows cover a great distance in every direction. To the
northwest the land gently slopes down into a verdant flower-clad valley where a
glimmering pond can be seen. To the northeast the grasslands turn into a level
marshy region where sedges and grasses dominate the murky landscape. A thin veil
of mist floats above the low-lying fields in the east.
name: Rolling Grasslands

desc:
Grassy hills dominate the landscape. When the wind blows, tall coarse grass
moves in sinuous waves seemingly endlessly. The deep valley north from here is
dotted with a myriad of flowers and a small pond can be spotted at one end of
the valley, beckoning the thirsty travellers. The rolling grasslands turn into
more level lands in the west.
name: Rolling Grasslands

desc:
Breaking away from the vibrant meadows of flowers in the north, tall shoots of
grass sway here quietly in the breeze. The air is moist and has a rich grassy
fragrance. Small puddles of water have formed in dips between the grasses. The
wide meadows of grass expand in every direction, turning slowly into rolling
hills in the eastern horizon.
name: Standing Meadows

desc:
With the exception of a huge blotch of yellowing grass, the surrounding
landscape appears primarily green and flourishing. Beneath the patch of yellow
grass lies a darkened portion of hard earth, probably the result of some mineral
which makes the ground less fertile. Other than the stained grass, the only
break to the verdant fields is a small trail cutting across the northwestern
horizon.
name: Yellowing Patch of Grass

desc:
As the trail continues after the interruption of a marred wooden bridge to the
west, it bends off southwards where it diminishes and eventually ends before a
large cliff. Thin grass growing on both sides of the trail quickly becomes vast
plains, and spreading out over the lands in lively nuances of yellow and green,
they contrast greatly against the dullness of the dirt road underfoot.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
A deep gully was formed here, probably ages ago, in the shaping of the world. A
few flowers and thick green-coloured grass now proudly defy the winds that
occasionally sweep through this small ravine. East and west, the ground is too
steep for even for the most skilled climber to reach without considerable
effort. Above, out of reach, a small bridge has been built. However, it looks
rather decripit and now creaks uninterruptedly.
name: Gully under a Rotten Bridge

desc:
Leading north and east, the trail cuts a diagonal path through the green
grassland. Well-worn and bumpy, the road seems often used but poorly maintained.
The southern landscape appears besieged by an overabundance of shrubs while the
ground slopes downwards to more undergrowth in the west. To the east, the trail
approaches a small bridge designed to pass over a noticeable gulch.
name: Bending Trail through the Plains

desc:
The flowing plains of green grass to the north and west slowly give way to
choppy, sparse grass and occasional bushes. Thin, stiff stems and coarse, oblong
leaves typify the bushes. To the south, the grass all but disappears, replaced
by plentiful shrubs and bushes. A tiny trail to the east vanishes into the
horizon in both the east and north.
name: Green Undergrowth near a Trail

desc:
Browning and brittle, the grass stands only a few inches off the ground. Most
appears like a brown trim at the edge of the carpet of green and healthy grass
to the north. To the south and southeast, the grassland thins even further and
becomes rocky and shrub-filled. A small collection of dilapidated buildings can
barely be seen on the southwestern horizon.
name: Thinning Grasslands

desc:
Thick grass to the north, east and west gives way to silky-plumed, squirreltail
grass. Gentle on the feet, the short grass provides magnificent nourishment for
the sizeable number of small animals that inhabit the prairie. Clumps of grass
and holes made by animals mar the surface. There is a small trail leading to a
collection of buildings to the south.
name: Near the Decrepit Quarters

desc:
Plentiful plants and thick, lush grass cover the floor of the valley. Copious
clusters of clover blend among the grass. A gentle gradient to the north leads
down to a bleak region on the plains. In stark contrast, a patch of vibrant
wheat grass rests atop the prairie to the southwest. To the southeast, a small
community comes into view, a dot on the far-away horizon.
name: Rich Countryside

desc:
Copious grass, standing over three feet tall, can be seen in all directions.
Amid the sea of green stalks in the north and east are flowers that provide a
sharp contrast to the verdant grass. A small ridge dominates the western horizon
above some leafy shrubs. Tracks through the high grass suggest something
travelled through here recently.
name: Among Deep Grasses

desc:
A profusion of wildflowers spreads across the surface of the treeless prairie.
Delicate, feathery leaves typify many of the plants. Although primarily purple
and orange, the flowers represent shades of the entire colour spectrum. To the
west, the rough grass possesses fewer colours while to the east the ground
appears very similar to that currently underfoot.
name: Untamed Wildflower Fields

desc:
The light brown colour of the barren and dry earth offers a vivid contrast to
the rich and pleasant land that typifies the vale. Tracks of animals remain
visible in the earth, indicative of the large amount of fauna that reside in
this area. In several places, fresh droppings reveal that animals travelled
through here recently. Far to the west, a long ridge stretches across the
horizon.
name: Treeless Dip on the Prairie

desc:
Brown, thick grass dominates the landscape. The ground seems unusually dry and
assuredly contributed to the dried-up condition of the grass. Sloping down to
the west, the plains sink to a harsh, grassless land. To the east, luscious
meadows are visible for several miles. Far to the south lies a barely
discernible trail leading into a small village.
name: Withered Plains

desc:
With the exception of a small trail visible on the northeastern horizon, lush
grass coats the ground of the surrounding region. Standing over a foot high in
places, the grass possesses sharp blades and a dark green colour. Small paths in
the grass reveal that animals pass through here occasionally. To the south, the
prairie begins to thin and slowly gives way to small rocks.
name: Luxuriant Grasses

desc:
Fields of greenish grass dominate the landscape. An occasional clump of dirt
causes irregular shifts in the height of the vegetation. In places, the grass
appears parted as though an animal recently traversed the prairie. A trail cuts
diagonally across the land from the northern horizon to the eastern one. To the
west and southwest lie fields of grass as far as the eye can see.
name: Plains alongside the Trail

desc:
An aged and dilapidated wagon lies partly buried in the roadway. Deep wheel ruts
remain visible on the road in the rear of the wagon. To the west lies a muddied
marsh, which assuredly contributed to the moist nature of the roadway and
trapped wagon. The trail appears less mud-covered to the north and south.
name: Sunken Trail

desc:
Yellow rye, standing nearly four feet tall, sways gently from side to side. Its
seed heads firm and white as pearls, they stand out vividly atop the dark green
grass that predominates here. A trail to the southwest provides the only break
to the vibrant grassland. Immediately to the south, a small bridge traverses a
small gorge covered by thick, green grass.
name: Fertile, Golden-green grass

desc:
A gathering of shrubs stands among the tall, verdant grass here. Characterized
by a knotty, woody, heavily branched stem. Diminutive, lightly serrated leaves
grow from the rod-like branches. To the north, bare granite walls form a small
natural fissure in the prairie. A decaying trail heads south through the thick
grassland up to a towering rock cliff in the distant south.
name: Brushy Plains

desc:
Thin, yellow blades of grass intermittently speckle the widespread carpet of
green stems. Orange and blue wildflowers provide extra colour to the landscape.
To the north, a small knoll masks the view of another tiny mound further in the
distance. If not for a small group of trees standing atop the distant hillock,
the second rise would blend in with the closer, grass-covered elevation.
name: Verdant Fields of Grass

desc:
Countless wildflowers grow amidst the flowing rows of tall grass. Flowers of
every imaginable colour can be seen dotting the moist meadows. These colourful
fields seem to stretch on for miles to the east, while to the south the lands
get more plain again. The land slopes slightly in the east to a verdant flower
filled valley.
name: Florid Meadows

desc:
The verdant green valley floor is painted in a variety of colours with countless
flowers. Some bushes grow scattered on the steep hillsides. A small pond of
sparkling clear water has formed at the far end of the valley, providing
refreshing water to the wildlife and the occasional traveller. Some boulders
near the pond provide a good resting place for the weary.
name: A Flourishing Valley

desc:
As the moist flower-clad meadows grow lush in a line of downs ahead, the grassy
lands continue in endless waves of green. In the west the land slopes steeply to
a verdant, colourful valley. Down in the valley a glimmering pond can be seen.
To the east the lands turn into marshy, bleak plains. A stream can be seen
cutting across the country.
name: Undulating Meadows

desc:
The miry lands are predominated by sedges and cattails. Where the cover of the
mist floating above the marsh breaks away, the wet, soft and treacherous ground
can be seen. The yielding land makes travelling slow and often precarious. A
serpentine stream cuts through the marsh in the east while to the west the marsh
gradually turns into verdant meadows and rolling grasslands.
name: Marshy Plains

desc:
In places where the veil of mist breaks away, the treacherous mire underneath
can be seen. Numerous cattails with their brown furry heads sway silently above
the cover of the mist. The progress through the marsh is precarious and soon
becomes tiresome. To the west the mist gradually thins out and the marsh turns
into rolling grasslands.
name: Marshy Plains

desc:
In the east the grasslands slowly descend to marshy plains where a silvery
stream can be seen cutting across the country. The treeless golden-green slopes
and downs continue in other directions in sinuous waves of grass. Far to the
southwest a particularly verdant and flourishing valley can be seen; the
glimmering waters of a pond at the nearest end of the valley attracting animals
and wanderers alike.
name: Rolling Grasslands

desc:
Tall coarse grass grows in every direction on undulating hills and valleys. Only
an occasional bush or a solitary tree break the monotony of the seemingly never
ending waves of grass. Far to the west and north the landscape slowly turns into
level plains while the eastern and southern horizons are predominated by the
hilly landscape. In the south the land abruptly slopes down into a verdant
flower-clad valley.
name: Rolling Grasslands

desc:
The broad expanse of grassland continues in all directions as far as the eye can
see. The treeless monotony of the grasslands is broken by a rising hillock to
the north. Far beyond it glimmers the white head of Methedras, the last peak of
the Misty Mountains. Some fresh animal droppings speak of the presence of
wildlife in these plains. Far to the east the level lands turn into undulating
hills and valleys.
name: Wide Expanse of Grass

desc:
Although not incredibly high, this small, grassy mound offers the best view of
the surrounding countryside. Rolling plains predominate the landscape to the
east, south, and west. Far to the east, a wide river cuts north to south across
the undulating meads. A small trail traverses across the southwestern horizon.
To the north sits a small, tree-covered hillock providing a safe habitat for
songbirds and small animals.
name: Grassy Mound

desc:
Steep, rocky walls surround the area to the east and west. Large boulders and
loose rocks that crumbled in the north complete the formation of an unusual
gorge on the grassland floor. The gradient of the ravine makes passage to the
east, west, or south somewhat difficult. Padded earth lined with fur coats one
corner of the enclosure. Fresh air seems to sneak through holes in the fallen
rock to the north.
name: Tiny Ravine

desc:
Atop the green grasses of the prairie stand several luxurious bushes of wild
roses. When in bloom, the red roses provide a sharp contrast to the green so
ubiquitous throughout the area. Far to the northeast and east, rolling fields of
grass dominate the landscape while a small trail cuts across the western
horizon. Immediately east is a sharp drop into a small, but steep ravine.
name: Bushes of Wild Roses

desc:
Sloping downward slightly as it approaches from the west, the trail becomes
rougher as it stretches southward. The trail narrows slightly in order to bend
around a small cluster of oak trees. Beyond a group of bushes to the east, the
green plains stretch as far as the eye can see. More grassland dominates the
landscape to the southwest.
name: Narrowing Trail

desc:
Ruts and stones are commonplace atop the surface of the roadway. The condition
of the road seems comparable to the east while in the north it improves at the
intersection of several pathways. Beside the trail stand several large bushes.
The pointed shrubs, standing over three feet tall, possess leafy, smooth stems
and fuzzy, green leaves.
name: Along the Roughly-surfaced Trail

desc:
With the exception of a few lone maple trees standing atop the prairie, the
plains here are dominated by soft, dense grass. Gentle on the feet, the grass
provides ample nourishment for the numerous animals that live in these parts.
Bordered on the north and east by a small trail, the rich grassland continues on
to the south and west.
name: Bountiful Plains

desc:
Loose gravel, accumulated over years, covers the ground and makes footing shaky
here. Pebbles treacherously litter a small slope leading down from the road to
the north. The prairie to the south and west appears far less dangerous and
contains an abundance of magnificent, green grass.
name: Pebbled Incline beside the Road

desc:
In addition to bountiful grass, a single type of shrub marks the ground of the
prairie here. With dark-green leaves paired in groups of two to six, the tubular
flowers possess thick stems that are hairy near the base. To the south, the
plains end abruptly as they dip sharply to an austere countryside while to the
north the fields are topped by a group of alder trees.
name: Shrub-covered Fields

desc:
Undersized, lightly serrated leaves scratch at anything near the ground here.
Growing from small bushes of a light-coloured, rod-like plant, the leaves are
more of a nuisance than an impediment. In addition to their abrasive leaves, the
plants fill the area with a pleasant aroma. Similar scrub brush and pleasant
grass surround the immediate area.
name: Leafy, Fragrant Countryside

desc:
The lobed, prickly-edged leaves of the milk thistle dominate the area. Streaked
with conspicuous white veins, the leaves present a stark contrast to the green
grass on which they grow. In the distance to the north runs a small road.
Further beyond the road lies more grassland and some small foothills on the
northern horizon.
name: Bush-laden Plains

desc:
An isolated group of trees sits atop the yellow-green grass of the prairie,
offering a meager amount of shade during the day. Diminutive tracks in the soil
reveal that tiny animals occasionally gather here. Beautiful grassland surrounds
this small group of trees on all sides with a road cutting through the prairie
to the north.
name: A Meager Copse

desc:
Bending northwards and eastward, the trail cuts diagonally across the plains.
Although not in perfect condition, the road appears well maintained and well
travelled. Beside the road lies an assortment of tiny wildflowers. Amethyst-
purple sego lilies and the tube-like petals of a wild orange flower provide a
splash of colour to the dull brown and green hues that dominate this area.
name: Winding Trail

desc:
Traversing the land between rolling plains, the trail can easily be followed as
it runs east to west. Other than an occasional rut or hollow, probably caused by
a small animal, the pathway remains relatively flat and straight. In the
distance in both the east and west, the trail appears to bend north and south,
respectively. To the north lies a field of clover while to the south lies
gentle, wavy grass.
name: Straight Trail through the Prairie

desc:
Tracks of hooves and other beasts suggest the route here is well travelled. The
road splits in three directions as the trails heading north and south merge with
a road approaching from the west. To the east lies fertile, golden-green grass.
Similar grassland can be seen bordering the roads to the south and north.
name: Intersection of the Trails

desc:
With the exception of green fields to the east, a collection of roads meets in
the west and forms a border in all directions. The ground slopes down slightly
from the trail in the south, west, and north, leaving this area littered with
small stones that were washed down the inclines over time by rain and snow.
Amidst the collection of rocks, clumps of grass poke their heads up reaching for
the sky.
name: Beside the Intersection of Trails

desc:
Among the thinning grass lay an assortment of wild mushrooms. The cream-colour
stems of the mushrooms slowly give way to a tinged pale pinkish top. Sticky to
touch, the mushroom tops possess a mild odour barely noticeable to passersby. On
the distant horizon in the north, west, and south, a rough trail cuts across the
grassy prairie.
name: Amidst a Mushroom Field

desc:
Copious grass conceals the earthen floor of the vale. Similar to the grasslands
in the north, small plants blend among the grass. A patch of auburn mushrooms
spots the prairie in the west while the grass to the east runs up to a small
knoll topped by a ring of trees. To the south, loose rocks form a stony
outcropping atop the prairie.
name: Grassland beside a Cleft

desc:
About a dozen maple trees stand clustered closely atop a small knoll. The boughs
of the trees interlace and provide cover from the sky. On the outskirts of the
trees, small plants with thick, coarse-toothed leaves predominate and provide
ample food resources for the number of animals that make the small copse their
home. To the south lies a treeless, grassy knoll while to the west lie more flat
grasslands.
name: Small Coppice on a Low Knoll

desc:
The most prominent feature here is a rising hillock standing only slightly
higher than the surrounding grasslands. The grass effectively hides any dips and
burrows in the hill. A sturdy tree stands firmly on one side of the grassy
mound, casting a gentle shadow over a patch of white poppies. The grass-covered
fields dominate the view for miles all around the hill.
name: A Rising Hillock

desc:
The plains of grass continue in all directions, the level fields turning into
rolling lands in the east and south. Some bushes flourish nearby, their red
flowers giving off a rich scent to the air. A small burrow can be made out under
their branches. Tracks of some animals can be seen on the moist ground near the
bushes.
name: Wide Expanse of Grass

desc:
The rolling grass-covered hills decline gradually to more level plains in the
north. Long treeless slopes and grassy hills stand in line after line for many
miles in other directions. Far in the east the land gradually slopes down to a
marshy plain where a stream can be seen cutting across the horizon from north to
south.
name: Rolling Grasslands

desc:
Sedges and cattails predominate the marsh, eerily swaying back and forth in a
veil of mist that softly caresses the wet ground underfoot. The precarious lands
make the progress tedious. A serpentine stream snakes through the marshland in
the east. To the west the miry land turns into rolling plains of grass.
name: Marshy Plains

desc:
A thin cover of mist floats above the sodden ground that yields underfoot. Tall
grasses and tufted marsh plants grow in patches all around. The miry lands
stretch far to the south while in the west the marsh turns into green, verdant
plains. A stream flowing southwards runs across the plains and passes east of
here.
name: Sodden Fields

desc:
The broad and level golden-green expanse of grass continues for many miles to
the western horizon whereas to the east the lands abruptly turn into a marshy
plain. Rolling hills dominate the southerly lands. Tall coarse grass effectively
hides all possible landmarks making it difficult to orientate. Not a single tree
can be seen for miles around.
name: Wide Expanse of Grass

desc:
A broad treeless expanse of grasslands can be seen in all directions. The
fertile soil of the plains is covered with tall coarse grasses that silently
sway when the wind blows. Droppings on the ground near some low-lying shrubbery
speak of the animals that live on the plains. Some distance to the north a farm
can be seen, apparently abandoned.
name: Wide Expanse of Grass

desc:
Far to the north and east all that is visible is grass, shrubs, and spots of
water where the thick grass fails to cover the land. Flowers of all colours
spring up here and there throughout the fields, sparkling among the rows of
grass and weed. The air here is thick and fragrant. The grass becomes slightly
thinner to the west while a small hill can be seen south of here.
name: Wide Expanse of Grass

desc:
The grass thins noticeably as the tips of granite bedrock become conspicuous.
The coarse crabgrass presents a stark contrast to the thick, tall grass
prevalent in all other directions. A few tangled blackberry brambles have
managed to take root among the outcroppings. To the south, a small cluster of
maple trees tops a knoll.
name: White Granite Outcroppings

desc:
The light, dry soil provides an extremely fertile environment for varied
foliage. One plant, standing erect with a many-branched, cylindrical stem,
possesses light, feathery leaves. The verdant plains dominate the view in every
direction with a single road traversing the northwestern horizon serving as the
only break in the sea of green.
name: Luxurious Prairie

desc:
Narrow, long blades of grass with tapering points coat the floor of the vale.
Mixed with the longer grass is a shorter variety possessing elongated, barbed
awns with a sparse coating of hair along their veins. Large fields of brown and
cream-coloured mushrooms are visible to the south.
name: Prickly Grass

desc:
Bending west and north, the trail cuts diagonally across the green plains.
Clumps of scattered brush line the sides of the path. Although not a large road,
tracks upon its surface indicate that something occasionally utilizes it. Beyond
the brush that borders the road, grassy plains are visible upon the northwestern
and southeastern horizons.
name: Trail across the Plains

desc:
Sloping gently from the east, the narrow, worn trail continues to the south up
an even smaller incline. Handfuls of tiny flowers grow beside the roadway in
several places. To the south, the trail meets a barely discernible path coming
from the west. Fields of green are visible beyond the boulder-strewn grass to
the north.
name: Winding Trail on the Prairie

desc:
Massive clusters of clover intermingle with the thick grass that is prevalent
underfoot. The bulky clover measures up to several inches across in width and
stands more than a foot high in some dense patches. To the west and south, a
small trail zigzags across the plains. In the north and east, vibrant fields of
green grass cover the earth comprising the plains.
name: Fields of Clover

desc:
The roadway marches roughly south and west as in several places, craggy
outcroppings force the road to diverge from a straight path. The surface of the
road could use some repair but remains largely intact. To the north small holes
and bushes provide the only interruption to the endless grassland. Far to the
west, the road appears to split and head back to the north as well as the south.
name: Zig-zagging Path through the Plains

desc:
Wild berry plants covered with sharp thorns and wind-blown leaves partially
cover the roadway. A small coppice to the south assuredly provided the foliage
atop the roadway. To the north lie rolling prairies as far as the eye can see.
The road becomes more visible to the west and east as no undergrowth or leaves
obscure the roadway.
name: Vegetation-cloaked Trail

desc:
Intermittent blades of grass thrust through the cobbled roadway, revealing the
often barren nature of the pathway. Stretching both north and east, the trail
provides one of the few visible breaks to the otherwise prevailing plains. The
only other anomaly, the River Isen, cuts a path through the flatlands several
miles to the west.
name: Bumpy Trail over the Prairie

desc:
Coarse timbers form the frame of this small structure. Between the timbers lies
mud, designed to provide further protection from inclement weather. Rough-hewn
wooden boards comprise the floor of the structure. In several places, hay lies
atop the rough boards. Farming implements line a rustic rack on the southern
wall.
name: Inside a Decrepit Dwelling

desc:
At one time, this diminutive shed housed many of the implements needed to run a
self-sufficient farmstead. Deep nicks and scratches mar the surface of a long
bench along the southern wall. A hefty, circular block of wood stands along the
eastern wall while a small doorway to the north leads to a courtyard occupied by
weeds and other scrub brush.
name: Storage Shed beside the Neglected Courtyard

desc:
This dwelling must have been built with two purposes in mind because no sane
craftsman would have left the eastern wall half open. The eastern wall simply
stops half way between the north and south walls, leaving a wide opening. A
massive animal hide hangs from a nail near the opening, apparently a makeshift
door. A very large stove has been built into the eastern wall as well, allowing
someone to use it both within the house and without. A proliferation of metal
working tools hangs upon the walls inside this house and just beyond the door a
black anvil rests. Along the western wall, two soot covered beds and a small
table can just be discerned under the pile of filthy clothes.
name: A Large Dwelling

desc:
Inside this small circle of homes, one is witness to a rare site in these hotly
contested plains, a Rohir homestead. A few small homes have been built in a
circle surrounding a communal well, a few young trees and some strategically
placed benches. All the houses are made from large flat stones and split logs,
apparently to conserve the hard-to-come-by wood. The thatched roof of every
house appears well cared for. However the real connection between all these
houses is not their appearance but their clotheslines. Strung up between every
house are clotheslines, connecting one neighbour to the next. Then each house
has put up a second line between their house and the nearest tree. The whole of
the village takes on the look of a massive spider web.
name: The Village Square

desc:
Entering this small dwelling, a multitude of smells reels your senses. A large
variety of wild flowers and herbs grow from hanging planters, hooked into the
ceiling by sturdy ropes. Long shelves are covered in dried or drying spices,
stored for winter use. A stout wooden table sits in the centre of this one room
home, surrounded by four simple chairs. A large but tattered cloth hangs across
the western side of the house, forming a partition for the sleeping area.
name: In a Small Farm House

desc:
The darkness swarms in around you as you descend into the channel. The walls of
the channel are smooth from centuries of rushing water. Faint shapes can be
discerned in the inky blackness, a pair of eyes bulging here and a flick of a
tail there. The channel continues east into the abyss.
name: Underwater Channel

desc:
The water becomes colder, as beneath you the floor of the tunnel drops away into
the inky blackness. The silvery flickers of small fish darting in, out, and
between small nooks and crannies provide the only source of illumination. The
walls of the tunnel are smooth from the erosion of centuries with only small
pockets to provide anything to hold on to.
name: Underwater Tunnel

desc:
The walls of the tunnel are covered in small holes and pockets, from some of
which eyes, glinting in the blackness can be seen. The floor of the tunnel is
covered with a fine dark sand absorbing any light that might manage to penetrate
the gloom. The water is cold and sharp, biting your exposed flesh. The ceiling
opens up leading towards what must be the surface and to the east the tunnel
continues on into the dark depths.
name: Underwater Tunnel

desc:
The submerged tunnel comes to a dead end, rocks closing in and creating passages
too narrow for you to pass. Cracks and crevices line the walls, dark and glum in
the icy cold water. Above, a piece of dark wood is jammed into the ceiling.
name: End of the Underwater Tunnel

desc:
This small chamber shows evidence of once being water-filled. The smooth and
rounded walls together with piles of small, round stones on the floor are plain
to see. However, traces of men are also evident, as sections of the wall have
been chiselled to enlarge the cavern. In places, the workers have been slightly
careless, and piles of rocks show where parts of the ceiling has caved in.
name: Crumbling Chamber

desc:
This massive chamber was carved out of rock as dark as wet earth. The cavern was
created by quickly flowing water, evidenced by the smooth, round edges on most
surfaces. On the northern wall, a ruined tapestry has been nailed into the rock
and is surrounded by deep etchings. A small lantern hangs from a tiny
outcropping near the tattered wall covering. Broken pots, half-eaten food and
battered clothing are just a few of the items that can be spotted in the trash
that litters the floor near the light.
name: A Wide Cavern

desc:
Rock walls, worn smooth by eons of water erosion, rise into the darkness. The
ground quickly slopes down to the west and small rivulets of water form an
unusual path into the darkness. On the southern wall, an extrusion in the wall
mars its near pristine surface.
name: Waterlogged Cavern

desc:
A solid wall of rock blocks passage to the north. The cold water is beginning to
sap your strength and your vision blurs in the icy blackness. Eyes glint in the
darkness as the inhabitants of this tunnel move around. The tunnel continues to
the south and the walls to either side are covered with shelves about a foot
apart. This tunnel has been purposely made and then flooded some time later.
name: At a Rock Wall

desc:
The catacombs twist north and east. Many of the shelves adorning the walls of
the tunnel have remains of old soldiers. Within some of the skeletons, eyes can
be seen twinkling in the darkness. The floor of the catacomb has been worn
smooth by the passage of time and water. Swimming in the icy water is rapidly
depleting your strength, above you the ceiling is cracked in places.
name: A Twisting Tunnel

desc:
The catacomb bends to the south and west here. The remains of countless warriors
lie in the shelves adorning the walls. The water here is cold and dark, piercing
your clothes and sending a chill to your bones, sapping your strength every
second you remain.
name: Bend in the Catacomb

desc:
The darkness seems to engulf you in the icy waters of the catacombs. The walls
are lined with shelves from floor to ceiling, within each shelf a solitary
skeleton rests. The skeletons of past warriors seem to watch you pass with an
eerie solace, as if they know you may get out of here alive but they will surely
not. The catacombs lead off north and east into the dark water.
name: A Dark Catacomb

desc:
This section of the catacombs, now flooded, was hidden away for a purpose. The
skeletons lying in the shelves that adorn the walls have the armour of officers
and leaders. This section of the catacombs was for the higher ranking
Numenoreans and was hidden away to prevent looting. The catacombs continue to
the west and a ladder leading to the surface is visible on the eastern wall.
name: Flooded Catacombs

desc:
An icy pool of water forms the floor of the tunnel. Treading the cold water is
beginning to sap your strength. Looking down it is apparent that your feet can't
touch the bottom. There is a ladder on the western wall leading up with the base
of it immersed somewhere below. All around you are shelves containing the
remains of many Numenoreans, these catacombs were built some time before they
were flooded.
name: Under the Altar

desc:
A large, domed tomb opens up before you. Occupying the centre is a large altar,
its sides and top etched with runes in a language lost eons ago. The altar is
black and seems to warp the space around it. The walls are lined with shelves,
each containing the remains of a once great warrior. The remains replete with
weapons and armour now rusted beyond repair. A tunnel opens up to the south and
seems to be the only exit from this eerie place.
name: Tomb Chamber

desc:
An eerie chill catches your breath. All the walls of this tomb are covered with
shelves from floor to ceiling. Looking closer you can see each shelf contains
the remains of a once great warrior replete with armour and weapons. The tomb
was used to bury the fallen from the battlefield above. Silence rules this tomb
and only the very brave dare utter a sound. The shelved walls continue on to a
chamber to the north.
name: Battlefield Tomb

desc:
This large tent is the sleeping area for the Rohir soldiers of the camp.
Sleeping mats lie in bundles along the sides, ready to be used. Small chests
filled with personal belongings stand in a corner. The tent is rather dark, and
only lit by a small fireplace in one of the corners.
name: Large Tent

desc:
This tent contains the sleeping area for the soldiers of the camp. The area can
house a large number of men at the same time, but currently, all the sleeping
mats are rolled up and stacked along one of the walls. The thick fabric of the
tent does not allow much light to enter.
name: Large Tent

desc:
Lush grasslands to the north and east give way gradually to the coarse grass
underfoot. The choppy grass and small rocks conceal the rich soil of the vale.
In addition to the grass and rocks, several masses of decaying leaves rest
against the larger stones, pushed together by intermittent winds. The remains of
a decaying village to the west appear worn over time by the harsh sun and
pounding rain that characterise the Rohan plains. A very faint, barely
discernable footpath leads from the village towards the underbrush to the east.
name: East of a Dilapidated Village

desc:
Empty stalls line the northern wall. In days gone past, the stalls assuredly
housed the animals that allowed this small hamlet to flourish. Now the stalls
lie empty and badly maintained. Several planks hang loosely from old nails.
Extremely aged hay and silage clutter the floor of the barn and stalls. To the
south, an empty doorway opens to a weed-filled courtyard.
name: Unkempt Barn

desc:
Hot, humid air predominates inside this dark, gloomy cave. Coarse, rigid granite
comprises the cavern walls. Clumps of fur line the floor of the cave along the
southern wall, indicating that some creature makes this cavern its home. A small
fissure to the north leads out into verdant, green grassland.
name: Murky Cave beneath the Mountain

desc:
A single torch hangs from the northern wall, emitting a soft, flickering light
that reflects off the quartz in the rock. This small illumination source
brightens the entire tiny room. The rounded cavern floor is caked in a thick,
viscous mud. The surface of the mud is marred by a small protuberance. Barrels,
rugs, foodstuffs and a variety of obviously stolen items obscure the rest of the
floor. The air carries a slightly dank smell, as if fresh air was here at some
point but became stale not too long ago.
name: A Tiny Round Niche

desc:
Earthen walls completely surround this small, enclosed basement. At one time,
this room undoubtedly served as a root cellar for the home above. Carved into
the dirt walls, small shelves line the western and southern walls. A few broken
jars lie atop the rustic shelves.
name: Beneath the Ramshackle Dwelling

desc:
The road narrows considerably as it passes over an undersized bridge. Designed
to cross a small gully where water collects during rainstorms, the bridge
remains in place and barely useable. Rotten in places, the wooden roadway bends
and creaks when crossed. From opposite ends of the bridge, the road continues to
the west and east.
name: Decaying Bridge over a Gully
